tasks.title: Extract useFocusTrap hook + add focus traps to drawer, toolbar overlays, and NotificationCenter
tasks.description:
Modal.tsx already has an excellent focus trap implementation (lines 75-103) with FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR, Tab/Shift+Tab wrapping, and focus save/restore. Extract this into a reusable `useFocusTrap` hook, then apply it to the 3 overlays that currently lack focus containment.

**Step 1 — Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts`:**
Extract the focus trap logic from Modal.tsx into a hook: `useFocusTrap(containerRef: RefObject<HTMLElement>, isActive: boolean)`. The hook should:
- Save `document.activeElement` on activation, restore on deactivation (like Modal lines 37-50)
- Auto-focus the first focusable element on activation (like Modal line 42-44)
- Handle Tab/Shift+Tab wrapping within the container (like Modal lines 75-103)
- Export `FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR` as a named export for reuse

**Step 2 — Refactor Modal.tsx:**
Replace the inline focus trap logic in Modal.tsx with `useFocusTrap(contentRef, isOpen)`. Remove the duplicated `FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR`, `previousFocusRef`, the save/restore useEffect, and the `handleKeyDown` callback. Import `useFocusTrap` and `FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR` from the new hook. Modal should behave identically after refactor.

**Step 3 — Apply to mobile drawer in AppLayout.tsx:**
The mobile drawer (lines 404-431) has `role="dialog"` and Escape handling but no focus trap and no focus restore. Add `useFocusTrap(drawerRef, drawerOpen)`. The drawer already has a `drawerRef` (line 103). When the drawer closes, focus should return to the hamburger button — add a ref for it.

**Step 4 — Apply to ProjectToolbar overlays:**
Template overlay (role="dialog", lines 315-362): Already has `previousFocusRef` and auto-focus. Add `useFocusTrap` using a ref on the overlay div. Remove the manual focus management since the hook handles it.
Export overlay (role="menu", lines 364-437): Already has arrow key nav and `previousFocusRef`. Add `useFocusTrap` to contain Tab. Keep the arrow key handler since it's menu-specific behavior the hook doesn't cover.

**Step 5 — Apply to NotificationCenter.tsx:**
The notification panel (line 132: `role="menu"`) has arrow key nav and click-outside but no focus trap and no focus restore. Add `useFocusTrap(panelRef, true)` — it's always "active" when mounted since the component only renders when open. Add focus restore to the trigger button when closing (the trigger is in AppLayout, so pass an `onClose` that handles this).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: New useFocusTrap hook exists at apps/web/src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts. Modal.tsx uses the hook (no duplicated trap logic). Tab key is contained within: mobile drawer, template overlay, export overlay, NotificationCenter. Focus restores to trigger element when each overlay closes. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build passes. Existing Modal behavior unchanged.

tasks.title: Responsive ProjectToolbar + task detail panel drawer on mobile
tasks.description: The ProjectToolbar and TaskDetailPanel have zero responsive behavior. On narrow screens the toolbar overflows and the 440px detail panel causes horizontal scroll. **ProjectToolbar responsive layout:** The main toolbar row (line 201) uses `flex items-center justify-between` with no wrapping. The view toggle block alone is ~360px (6 buttons). Fix: 1. Wrap the toolbar in `flex-wrap gap-2` so items flow to a second row on narrow screens 2. Hide the view toggle text labels on small screens: wrap each button label in `Backlog` so only icons show on mobile. The icons are already present (`IconList`, `IconBoard`, etc.). 3. Hide the search input (`w-48`) below `sm:` breakpoint — the ⌘K quick search in the sidebar/top bar is the mobile search path. Use `hidden sm:block` on the SearchInput wrapper. 4. The left side breadcrumb ("← Projects / Name") can truncate the project name with `truncate max-w-[120px] sm:max-w-none`. **TaskDetailPanel as mobile drawer:** Currently rendered as a `w-[440px] flex-shrink-0` sidebar in ProjectDetail.tsx (line 447). On mobile this should be a full-screen overlay drawer: 1. At `md:` and above: keep current behavior (fixed 440px sidebar) 2. Below `md:`: render as a fixed overlay (`fixed inset-0 z-40`) with the panel taking full width. Add a backdrop and close button (X in top-right). Slide in from the right using `translate-x-0` / `translate-x-full` with transition. 3. The close trigger: the existing `setSelectedTask(null)` handler. Clicking the backdrop should also close. 4. In ProjectDetail.tsx, conditionally render: `{d.selectedTask && <>{/* md: inline panel, }` **Sidebar notification preferences fix:** The notification preferences button is hidden when the sidebar is collapsed (it's inside the `isExpanded &&` block in AppLayout sidebarContent). This is a 1-line fix but AppLayout is Worker 1's file. Skip this — note it as a follow-up for after Worker 1's task merges. IMPORTANT: Worker 1 is also touching ProjectToolbar.tsx for focus traps. Your changes are to the responsive layout (flex-wrap, hidden classes, view toggle) which are different from Worker 1's focus trap additions. However, there may be merge conflicts. Complete your task quickly and mark complete — if Worker 1 hasn't merged yet, the reviewer will handle rebase.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar: view toggle shows icons-only below sm breakpoint, search hidden below sm, breadcrumb truncates, toolbar wraps on narrow screens. Task detail: renders as full-screen overlay drawer below md breakpoint with backdrop + close button. Desktop behavior unchanged. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build passes.

tasks.title: Remaining Card/Badge adoption + dead code cleanup + useAsyncData hook
tasks.description:
Three cleanup items that don't overlap with Workers 1-2:

**1. Remaining Badge/Card adoption (4 files):**

- **DependencyBadge.tsx** (lines 57-58): Uses inline `bg-amber-100 text-amber-700` and `bg-green-100 text-green-700`. Import Badge and replace: blocked state → `<Badge variant="warning" size="sm">`, unblocked → `<Badge variant="success" size="sm">`. Keep the custom tooltip/interactive logic.
- **KanbanBoard.tsx** (lines 6-11): `COLUMN_ACCENTS` array has `pillClass` strings like `bg-blue-100 text-blue-700`. These are used as CSS classes applied to column header pills. Refactor: instead of `pillClass` string, store a `variant` key (`'neutral' | 'info' | 'purple' | 'success' | 'accent'`) and render `<Badge variant={accent.variant}>` at the usage site.
- **KanbanBoard.tsx** (line 251): Task card uses inline card pattern `bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-lg p-3 shadow-sm border...`. Replace with `<Card padding="sm" className="shadow-sm border-l-4 ...">`. Import Card.
- **SprintSection.tsx** (line 214): Assignee initial badge uses inline `bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700`. Since Badge doesn't have an `indigo` variant, either add one or use `className` override on Badge: `<Badge className="bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700 dark:bg-indigo-900/30 dark:text-indigo-400">`.
- **CodePreviewModal.tsx** (line 461): Subtask badge uses inline purple. Already imports Badge — just replace with `<Badge variant="purple" size="sm">`.

**2. Badge `pink` variant:**
Add to Badge.tsx: `pink: 'bg-pink-100 text-pink-700 dark:bg-pink-900/30 dark:text-pink-400'`. Update `BadgeVariant` type. This differentiates `design-tool` from `ai-model` in TaskDetailPanel (both currently purple).

**3. useAsyncData hook + dead code cleanup:**
BurndownChart.tsx and Projects.tsx both have an unused `fetchData`/`fetchProjects` useCallback left over from lint fixes, followed by a duplicate async-await pattern in useEffect. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useAsyncData.ts`:
```typescript
function useAsyncData<T>(fetcher: () => Promise<T>, deps: unknown[]): { data: T | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null; retry: () => void }
```
The hook should handle: loading state, error state, cancellation on unmount/dep change, and a `retry()` function. Then refactor BurndownChart and Projects to use it, removing the dead callbacks and duplicated useEffect patterns.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: DependencyBadge, KanbanBoard (pills + card), SprintSection (assignee), CodePreviewModal (subtask) all use Badge/Card. Badge.tsx has `pink` variant (8 total). useAsyncData hook exists and is used by BurndownChart + Projects. Dead fetchData/fetchProjects callbacks removed. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build passes.
